business.headline: Why KYC is everywhere and why it frustrates so many IndiansCKYC was meant to make identity verification a one-time process, but for most Indians, KYC remains a repeated, unavoidable step across platforms. This story unpacks why a system built to simplify verification still leaves users proving who they are, again and again.

The first 30 days of FY27: 7 smart money moves to make in AprilThe new financial year has just begun, and April is the time to take charge. The decisions you make now can shape your money journey for the rest of the year. In our new 'MONEY MAKEOVER' series, we break down simple, smart steps to get started right.

Tech layoffs: Is AI replacing jobs or are companies fixing overhiring?Job cuts have been reported across tech companies, showing that the trend is not limited to a single type of company. The trend has raised a key question. Are companies correcting overhiring from the past, or is artificial intelligence starting to reduce the need for certain roles?

DA hike: How much will your salary and pension increase this time?With the next dearness allowance (DA) hike expected soon, central government employees and pensioners are watching closely. Even a small rise can push up monthly income, but how much extra can you really expect this time?

Markets down, rupee weak. Why Iran war may now hit your home loanThe impact of Iran war has not been limited to Dalal Street. The rupee has also come under pressure as oil prices moved higher. Now, the ripple effect of the conflict could reach households, especially those with home loans.

Iran war hit your portfolio? Track these 4 signals to stay ahead on Dalal StreetFor investors, the bigger challenge is not just falling markets, but the confusion around what to track. Headlines are changing rapidly, with mixed signals on escalation, oil prices and supply disruptions. This makes it difficult to take clear investment decisions.

Getting 8.2% on small savings? Don't invest before these 5 key checksGetting returns of up to 8.2% on small savings schemes may sound like an easy win. But before you lock your money in, it's worth pausing to check a few basics that could impact your finances later.

Income tax filing 2026: Which 5 key links should every taxpayer check now?Filing your income tax return in 2026 may feel a bit different this time. With the portal revamped and new rules in place, knowing where to click can save both time and effort. Here are five key links you should not miss.

Layoffs everywhere, but this company says no job cutsThe company has said it will not cut jobs despite ongoing global uncertainty. It has committed to retaining its workforce and continuing salary payments without delay.
Good Friday 2026: Is stock market open or closed today?Both stock exchanges, the Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE) and the National Stock Exchange (NSE), are closed on April 3 on account of Good Friday.

Why buying diamonds is expensive but fails as an investmentThe biggest issue with diamonds is the resale value. Unlike gold, which can be sold easily at market-linked prices, diamonds do not have a strong resale market.

Govt puts mandatory 60% free airline seat rule on hold amid jet fuel price riseThe government has put on hold its directive requiring airlines to offer at least 60% of seats without additional charges, pending further review.

Economic costs are increasing every day: Dr Sajjid Chinoy on impact of Iran warDr Sajjid Z Chinoy, MD and Chief India Economist at JP Morgan, discussed the escalating economic impact of the war on global and Indian markets.

India imposes 25% cap on monthly jet fuel price increase for domestic flightsIndia imposes 25% cap on monthly jet fuel price increase for domestic flights, Reuters reports citing an official statement

Good Friday holiday: Will NSE and BSE be open or closed on April 3?The stock market holiday list for 2026 includes a total of 16 non-trading days, excluding weekends. With several holidays already behind us, there are still multiple breaks lined up over the coming months.

Gold slips over RS 5,000, silver plunges Rs 15,000: What's behind the sudden fall?Precious metals, often seen as safe bets in uncertain times, have unexpectedly slipped. The latest drop in gold and silver prices has left investors wondering what's driving the decline despite global tensions.
Sensex ends 185 points higher, Nifty above 22,700; HCLTech up 3%Sensex ends 185 points higher, Nifty above 22,700; HCLTech up 3%

Markets rebound: Sensex, Nifty recover losses after early crash. Here's whyEarlier in the day, both Sensex and Nifty had fallen nearly 2%, reacting to fresh comments from US President Donald Trump, who said the United States would hit Iran "extremely hard" in the coming weeks.

Form 15G, 15H gone: Will new Form 121 simplify TDS for taxpayers?Form 121 is a self-declaration that allows individuals to request that no TDS be deducted on certain types of income. This usually includes interest earned from bank deposits, post office schemes, or similar sources.

Will the new Income Tax website make filing returns simpler for you?The new website brings together all income tax-related information in one place. According to the department, it is easier to navigate, faster to use, and built to reduce confusion for taxpayers who often struggle with forms, rules and procedures.

entertainment.headline: Global first, desi later: Ramayana's bold, big-screen bet beyond Indian box office The Ramayana teaser signals a bold global-first rollout, with the makers positioning the epic for international audiences from the outset. Here's why this ambitious approach, backed by scale and storytelling, could redefine how Indian epics travel worldwide.

Dhurandhar 2 Day 17: Film crosses Rs 1,564 crore globally and Rs 1,179.30 in India Dhurandhar 2 continues its blockbuster run, crossing Rs 1000 crore in India gross and Rs 1,564 crore worldwide. Ranveer Singh's spy thriller sees a strong third-week boost.
Ranveer Singh recreates Akshaye Khanna's viral Dhurandhar line at NMACC gala. Watch Ranveer Singh recreated iconic dialogues from Dhurandhar at the NMACC gala, winning cheers from the audience.

Ranbir Kapoor as Rama looks a little harsh: TV's popular Lakshman, Sunil Lahri Actor Sunil Lahri shared his views on Ranbir Kapoor's portrayal of Lord Rama in Ramayana Part 1, amid mixed reactions to the teaser. While noting certain missing qualities, he expressed confidence that the actor will justify the role.

Rashmika cuts cake at airport, heads for birthday getaway with Vijay, family. Video Ahead of her birthday on April 5, Rashmika Mandanna cut a cake at the airport before flying out for a vacation on Saturday. Videos quickly went viral. Vijay Deverakonda and his family were also spotted, adding to the buzz around the celebrations.

Patriot trailer out: Mammootty-Mohanlal's film promises high-stakes spy action The trailer of Patriot brings Mammootty and Mohanlal together on screen after 18 years. The spy action drama promises an intense story around a controversial intelligence mission.

Dhurandhar, Chhaava and newcomer of 550 films: Anupam Kher with Ranveer and Vicky Anupam Kher's fun selfie with Ranveer Singh and Vicky Kaushal from NMACC's anniversary celebration grabbed attention online. The actor praised the duo's talent and dedication while calling himself a "newcomer" in a note.

Divya Dutta shares how she stood up to man harassing her sister: Whacked his wits Divya Dutta recalled a childhood incident where she confronted a man harassing her sister, crediting her mother for giving her the courage to stand up against wrongdoing. She was last seen in Chiraiya.

Malayalam director Ranjith gets 3-day police custody in assault case Malayalam director Ranjith has been sent to three-day police custody in a sexual assault case filed by an actor. The probe is being handled by a Special Investigation Team, while Keralam CM Pinarayi Vijayan said due process is being followed.

Dacoit trailer: Adivi Sesh, Mrunal Thakur lead a tale of love and betrayal The trailer of Dacoit promises a gripping tale of love, betrayal and revenge, led by Adivi Sesh and Mrunal Thakur. Packed with action and emotion, the film is set for a multilingual theatrical release on April 10.

Anurag Kashyap's Dev.D returns to theatres this April Dev.D, Anurag Kashyap's cult classic, is set to return to theatres this April. Known for its bold storytelling and memorable music, the film stars Abhay Deol, Kalki Koechlin and Mahie Gill.

Toaster trailer: Rajkummar Rao, Sanya Malhotra bring dark twist to everyday life Rajkummar Rao and Sanya Malhotra's Toaster trailer promises a quirky, chaotic story centred around an ordinary household item that leads to merry chaos. The films marks the debut as producer of actor and Rao's wife Patralekhaa.

Akshay Kumar joins Naagin 7 as Naag Guru, fans react to new promo. Watch Akshay Kumar makes a special appearance in Naagin 7 as the Naag Guru. The cameo has excited fans and coincides with the promotion of his upcoming film Bhooth Bangla, which has been postponed to April 17.

From 1,200 aspirants, how Sara Arjun bagged Dhurandhar opposite Ranveer Singh Casting director Mukesh Chhabra opened up about Sara Arjun's casting in Dhurandhar. He revealed that many actors vied for the role of Yalina opposite Ranveer Singh's Hamza.

Ranveer Singh brings full Bollywood chaos at NMACC bash, dances to Gallan Goodiyan Ranveer Singh's impromptu dance with Shreya Ghoshal and Shankar Mahadevan stole the show at NMACC's third anniversary. The trio danced to the popular track, Gallan Goodiyan.

Bloodhounds 2 review: Woo Do-hwan's show lands like a clean, decisive knockoutRelease Date:Fri Apr 03, 2026 Bloodhounds Season 2 returns with sharper action and stronger emotions, exploring brotherhood in the brutal world of underground boxing. The Korean thriller blends intense fights with heartfelt moments, keeping viewers hooked.

Vaazha 2 review: Bros, brawls and big feelings, a nostalgic ride that hits homeRelease Date:Thu Apr 02, 2026 A nostalgic, laughter-filled coming-of-age tale that is a perfect mixture of chaos, friendship, and emotion, the Malayalam film Vaazha 2 delivers a relatable and wholesome sequel that stands strong on its own.

Ranbir Kapoor confirms double role in Ramayana. Here's what his second role is Ranbir Kapoor confirmed he has a double role in Ramayana. Read on to know his second role apart from Lord Rama. Part 1 of the Nitesh Tiwari film is set for a Diwali release and the sequel next year.

Not a celebrity drama: Blake Lively says amid court battle, vows to fight back Blake Lively remains firm in her fight against "digital violence" despite a court dismissing most of her sexual harassment claims. She said online abuse and retaliation are widespread, and added that she is ready to tell her story in court next month.

general news.headline: Terminated: Trump claims Iran's military leaders killed in strike, shares videoUS President Donald Trump claimed that a "massive strike" in Tehran had killed several top Iranian military leaders, sharing a video on Truth Social purportedly showing the strike. Though the timing of the attack was not mentioned.

Iran flags 'contamination risk' after attack near nuclear plant, writes to UNIran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said repeated strikes in the vicinity of the country's only operational nuclear power plant posed grave human and environmental dangers for the wider region. He said the repeated attacks near the active Bushehr facility had created an "intolerable situation" and warned that any radiological release could have severe consequences beyond Iran's borders.

Brace up, India: Rain, hail and thunderstorms are arriving on SundayThe IMD has issued a high alert for Delhi-NCR on Sunday, April 5, as back-to-back western disturbances trigger a risk of thunderstorms and gusty winds reaching 50 kmph.

Never refused Islamabad visit: Iran on 'deadlock' in Pak-mediated talks with USIranian Foreign Minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi said Tehran has "never refused to go to Islamabad" for peace talks, but emphasised that any negotiations must deliver a "conclusive and lasting" end to what it calls an "illegal war."

With Rs 500 for a kg of LPG, chhotu cylinder turns big daddyFor migrants, bachelors and street vendors, the small 5-kg cylinder has been a lifeline. Now the Iran war-induced LPG crunch has pushed their illegal refill rate to Rs 400-500 a kg. And the legal 5-kg cylinders are hard to find. Truly, the chhotu has become the baap, or even the granddaddy of LPG cylinders.

Live: Italy's Meloni visits Saudi, Qatar, UAE as war with Iran rages onUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: Tensions in the Middle East escalated sharply on Saturday after Donald Trump warned Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz within 48 hours, threatening severe consequences if it fails to comply. Meanwhile, the International Atomic Energy Agency reported that one person was killed by debris following joint US-Israeli strikes near Iran's Bushehr nuclear facility. At the same time, Israeli forces carried out fresh air raids across several towns in southern Lebanon, intensifying cross-border hostilities. In the Gulf, the UAE Ministry of Defence said its air defences intercepted 23 ballistic missiles and 56 drones launched from Iran. The fallout has also hit energy supplies, with Iraq reporting a complete halt in Iranian gas deliveries to its southern regions. Follow IndiaToday.in for the latest updates.

Confident SRH lick their lips as LSG face tough test in bowler's graveyardIPL 2026, SRH vs LSG:Sunrisers Hyderabad will be looking to build on their win over Kolkata Knight Riders, while Lucknow Super Giants will be eager to bounce back from their defeat to Delhi Capitals.

UP woman gets husband killed by lover. Then a QR code on liquor bottle surfacesAfter a partially burnt body was positively identified, the police immediately formed five teams to solve the case. The investigators arrested the woman, her lover and his aide within 24 hours.

YC startup Delve in trouble, Indian-origin founders face open-source scandalDelve has parted ways with Y Combinator after the startup, led by two Indian-origin techies, was hit by controversy over allegations by an anonymous whistleblower, "DeepDelver," that it repackaged an open-source tool and tried to sell it as their own product.

If India tries to...: Pakistan minister warns of striking Kolkata in future warA Kolkata strike warning, talk of entering Indian homes, and no evidence to back claims. Pakistan's latest remarks come right after Rajnath Singh's strong message. What's behind this fresh escalation?

Regime change happened: Iran mocks US after top officials 'sacked'Several analysts on US television and online have suggested that US Army Chief of Staff General Randy George may have been removed due to opposition to a ground invasion, though he has not made any public statements.

Christina Koch breaks record as woman who travelled farthest from EarthNasa astronaut Christina Koch has officially become the farthest travelling woman in history aboard the Artemis 2 mission. The Orion spacecraft is currently heading for a historic lunar flyby.

Dhurandhar 2 Day 17: Film crosses Rs 1,564 crore globally and Rs 1,179.30 in IndiaDhurandhar 2 continues its blockbuster run, crossing Rs 1000 crore in India gross and Rs 1,564 crore worldwide. Ranveer Singh's spy thriller sees a strong third-week boost.

1,000-km cloud band shifts from Delhi-NCR, severe hail alert for SundayThe Western Disturbance shifts focus this Sunday. Severe thunderstorms and hail are expected in Jhansi, Gwalior and North Madhya Pradesh after Saturday rain.

A 1,100-year-old dam, a dying reservoir and the slow erasure of Delhi's AravallisThe 'lungs of Delhi' are no longer able to breathe. The biggest pollutants now are encroachment, illegal construction sites, and excessive human intervention in the biodiversity. The 2.5-billion-year-old Aravallis, including the five major ridges in Delhi, have never before seen such conditions. In its ground report, the India Today team witnessed the deteriorating state of Delhi's oldest dam and the Tomars waterworks.

Film insulting Vijay won't even last a day, warns TVK leaderTVK leader Aadhav Arjuna said attempts to degrade their leader through cinema were part of a political ploy and questioned who had given filmmakers the authority to make a movie targeting Vijay.

IPL Play of the Day: Parag's instinct, Archer's setup, Deshpande grit stunned GTIPL 2026, GT vs RR: Riyan Parag's bold call to back Jofra Archer and Tushar Deshpande paid off as Rajasthan Royals held their nerve to defend 11 in the final over and seal a tense win over Gujarat Titans.
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

48 hours before all hell will rain down: Trump warns Iran on Hormuz blockadeEarlier, Trump had extended the deadline to reopen the critical energy chokepoint by 10 days, pushing it to April 6 after expressing optimism about ongoing talks with Iran.

Ranbir Kapoor as Rama looks a little harsh: TV's popular Lakshman, Sunil LahriActor Sunil Lahri shared his views on Ranbir Kapoor's portrayal of Lord Rama in Ramayana Part 1, amid mixed reactions to the teaser. While noting certain missing qualities, he expressed confidence that the actor will justify the role.

UP woman, aiding grandson park Thar, crushed to death after he loses controlThe woman was guiding her grandson as the latter tried to park the car inside the premises of their house. After the vehicle went inside, the woman proceeded to close the gate. Video suggests that the driver lost control of the car as the gates were being closed.

US arrests ex-Iranian General Soleimani's relatives after Green Cards cancelledUS federal agents arrested the niece and grand-niece of late Iranian commander Qasem Soleimani after their Green Cards were revoked. The arrests came amid the ongoing US-Israel war with Iran, now in its sixth week with no signs of easing.

3 killed after traveller carrying 20 tourists veers off road in Himachal's KulluThree tourists died when a traveller vehicle fell in Kullu, Himachal Pradesh. Six to seven others were hospitalised, and rescue operations are ongoing, officials confirmed.

sports.headline: Ravi Bishnoi opens up on tough IPL comeback with RR, what went wrong at LSG IPL 2026: Ravi Bishnoi starred with a four-wicket haul to script RR's win over GT. The spinner opened up on his tough IPL phase and how he worked his way back.

IPL Play of the Day: Parag's instinct, Archer's setup, Deshpande grit stunned GT IPL 2026, GT vs RR: Riyan Parag's bold call to back Jofra Archer and Tushar Deshpande paid off as Rajasthan Royals held their nerve to defend 11 in the final over and seal a tense win over Gujarat Titans.

Confident SRH lick their lips as LSG face tough test in bowler's graveyard IPL 2026, SRH vs LSG:Sunrisers Hyderabad will be looking to build on their win over Kolkata Knight Riders, while Lucknow Super Giants will be eager to bounce back from their defeat to Delhi Capitals.

B***s of steel: Shastri leads praise for Parag's leadership after Ahmedabad win Riyan Parag's bold call at the death drew praise from Ravi Shastri and Irfan Pathan after Rajasthan Royals held their nerve to defend 211 and seal a thrilling win over Gujarat Titans in Ahmedabad.

GT vs RR: Ice-cool Rajasthan Royals win last-ball thriller in Ahmedabad to go top IPL 2026, RR vs GT: Rajasthan Royals did the basics right in Ahmedabad to clinch a gripping Saturday night thriller. After posting 210, they fought back through Ravi Bishnoi's four-wicket haul and a superb death-overs effort from Jofra Archer and Tushar Deshpande.

154.2 kph: GT pacer Ashok Sharma fires a rocket vs RR, enters IPL's fastest club Ashok Sharma announced himself as IPL 2026's fastest bowler with a stunning 154.2 kph delivery for Gujarat Titans against Rajasthan Royals on Saturday. The 23-year-old earned praise from Dale Steyn, who highlighted his smooth, powerful action and near-perfect front-leg brace.

Sameer Rizvi makes case to be DC's No.4, but Axar Patel wants to keep options open IPL 2026: Sameer Rizvi's 90 powers Delhi Capitals past Mumbai Indians, but Axar Patel says selection will remain flexible despite the youngster's strong performances.

Shubman Gill misses Rajasthan Royals clash in injury concern for Gujarat Titans IPL 2026, GT vs RR: Shubman Gill missed the clash against Rajasthan Royals due to a muscle spasm. Rashid Khan led the side in his absence, while Kumar Kushagra replaced him in the playing XI.

Sameer Rizvi blitz sinks Mumbai Indians in Delhi as DC make it two in a row IPL 2026, DC vs MI: Sameer Rizvi backed up his Lucknow masterclass with a masterful 90 at the Arun Jaitley Stadium to help Delhi Capitals make it two out of two. The home team chased 163 with ease, sealing a comprehensive six-wicket win over Mumbai Indians with two overs to spare.

Bangladesh cricket board moves to repair BCCI ties as president Bulbul under fire There is strong resentment in the Bangladesh cricket corridors about Animul Islam Bulbul's continuation, especially after Bangladesh pulled out of the men's T20 World Cup in India.

GT vs RR Highlights: RR victorious in last-ball thriller to win by 6 runs Gujarat (GT) vs Rajasthan (RR) Highlights, IPL 2026: Despite losing track in the final stretch, the Rajasthan Royals have managed to come out victorious, by six runs, to take them to the top of the table.

DC vs MI: Hitman Rohit Sharma plays bluff man, dupes Delhi into a wasted reviewIPL 2026: Rohit Sharma's cheeky ploy fooled Delhi Capitals into wasting a review, which he later admitted was deliberate. His entertaining knock, however, ended soon after, as Mumbai Indians were restricted to a below-par total at Arun Jaitley Stadium in New Delhi.

Pat Cummins heads home for a final scan, expected to join SRH on April 17Pat Cummins has returned to Australia for a final scan on his injured back and is expected to rejoin Sunrisers Hyderabad on April 17 after clearance from Cricket Australia. In his absence, Ishan Kishan has led the team as stand-in captain, with Abhishek Sharma as deputy

Watch: Magnus Carlsen agrees to selfie with opponent, then gets phone removedMagnus Carlsen shared a brief pre-match selfie with Alua Nurman before their Grenke Open clash, but quickly called an arbiter to enforce rules, highlighting chess's strict stance on devices.

Abuse me if you want, CSK are disappointing: Ashwin loses cool after PBKS lossIPL 2026: R Ashwin delivered an emotional assessment of CSK after their loss to PBKS. The former spinner said the team's drop in intensity was "painful to watch."

IPL, GT vs RR Preview: Boss Baby Sooryavanshi ready to rumble in AhmedabadIPL 2026: Expect fireworks at the Narendra Modi Stadium as 15-year-old prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi leads Rajasthan Royals' explosive top order against Gujarat Titans' star-studded lineup.

Mumbai Indians rest Hardik Pandya for Delhi Capitals clash, Suryakumar Yadav leadsIPL 2026, DC vs MI: Hardik Pandya has been rested for Mumbai Indians' first away game this season against the Delhi Capitals. In his absence, Suryakumar Yadav will lead the five-time winners.

Assam football legend Gilbertson Sangma dies at 71, leaves lasting legacyGilbertson Sangma, known as Assam's "Man with the Golden Boot", died at the age of 71 in his Guwahati residence. The forward played a key role in shaping the state's football legacy.

DC vs MI Highlights: Sameer Rizvi's special gives Delhi Capitals win by 6 wktsDelhi Capitals (DC) vs Mumbai Indians (MI) Highlights, IPL 2026: Sameer Rizvi's special performance leaves Mumbai Indians scratching their heads. Delhi Capitals make it two wins in a row after beating MI by 6 wickets.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is the best player I've seen: Jos Buttler praises RR wonderboyIPL 2026: GT star batter Jos Buttler heaped praise on 15-year-old RR wonderkid Vaibhav Sooryavanshi in a viral moment. The England star backed the youngster to dominate world cricket in the years ahead.

CSK's biggest concern for the rest of IPL 2026 will be bowling, warns Sunil GavaskarIPL 2026, CSK vs PBKS: Sunil Gavaskar said Chennai Super Kings' bowling remains a major concern after their defeat to Punjab Kings, adding that the batters will be under pressure to post big totals in most matches.

Ronaldo scores on injury return, leads Al Nassr to 5-2 win to stay topCristiano Ronaldo scored twice on his return from injury as Al-Nassr defeated Al-Najma 5–2. The win strengthened their position at the top of the Saudi Pro League standings.

Kohli 'RCB's 5th overseas player'? Hilarious interview leaves batter in splitsIPL 2026: Virat Kohli shared a light moment over being called RCB's "fifth overseas player." The RCB star responded with humour while continuing his strong IPL 2026 form.

Jos Buttler just one innings away from best: GT coach backs star batterIPL 2026: GT assistant coach Aashish Kapoor has backed Jos Buttler to bounce back despite his recent dip in form. Kapoor believes the England star is just one innings away from his best.

IPL 2026: How Ricky Ponting transformed PBKS into a six-hitting forceIPL 2026: Ambati Rayudu revealed Ricky Ponting's unique squash-ball trick behind better six-hitting. The method may explain PBKS's rise as one of IPL's most explosive batting sides.

Shreyas Iyer fined Rs 24 lakh for 2nd over rate offence. Is he facing ban?IPL 2026: Shreyas Iyer has been fined Rs 24 lakh for PBKS' slow over-rate against CSK. The penalty comes despite a dominant chase that saw Punjab outclass Chennai at Chepauk.

Two bad innings don't define Sanju Samson: CSK coach defends struggling openerIPL 2026: CSK head coach Stephen Fleming has backed Sanju Samson despite his slow start in IPL 2026. The coach insisted two innings are not enough to judge a proven match-winner.

IPL Play of the Day: Chatur, chalak, chanchal Chahal chokes CSK at ChepaukCSK vs PBKS: Yuzvendra Chahal's set of 12 balls derailed Chennai Super Kings hopes of winning the first game of the season. In our IPL Play of the Day, we discuss how the wily old spinner was able to turn the game on its head.

Shirts or sponsor boards? Why IPL jerseys do not look good at allAn overload of sponsor logos, inconsistency in designs and sticking to two colours have made the IPL jerseys look functional and not iconic. The fans feel the disconnect and opt for counterfeit ones, rather than investing heavily on current IPL jerseys.

DC vs MI preview: Buoyant Delhi Capitals look to stop mighty Mumbai at homeIPL 2026, DC vs MI preview: Building on the momentum of a rare winning start, the mighty Mumbai Indians arrive in Delhi with Mitchell Santner's inclusion set to bolster an already formidable unit. However, the Capitals will be no pushovers in this high-octane afternoon clash at the Arun Jaitley Stadium.

Learn to chase like Kohli: Sehwag's advice to Raghuvanshi after KKR collapseVirender Sehwag urged Angkrish Raghuvanshi to learn the art of finishing from Virat Kohli after a promising knock ended in a costly run-out during KKR's failed 267 chase.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
